rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Rescheduling Of Hearing On Mtn To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories From Benjamin Warner
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 9, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, LLC's (Rescheduled) Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories From Benjamin Warner. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. On October 24, 2013, the parties stipulated to this motion being heard by Pro Tem Judge Kane. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to January 9, 2014 due to stay issued on November 21, 2013. Parties to meet and confer and provide by email to rkane1089@aol.com a joint statement of matters still at issue by noon on January 6, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH ALVARDO VS. JOSEPH MCGOVERN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Supplemental Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 9, 2013, Line 1: PLAINTIFF JOSEPH ALVARDO'S Motion For Supplemental Complaint Plaintiff's "Motion for Supplemental Complaint" is denied without prejudice to refilling another motion.. The Court understands the motion to be a request to amend the complaint. The motion is denied because Plaintiff's motion does not comply with the requirements California Rule of Court 3.1324. Plaintiff did not attach the proposed amended pleading that he seeks to file. (CRC 3.1324(a).). Plaintiff did not attach a separate declaration under penalty of perjury. (CRC 3.1324(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. MITCHELL ROSENFELD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records Served Upon Custodians Of Records For Old Republic Title Company, Fidelity National Title Company And Pacific Realty Exchange; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 9, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANTs MITCHELL ROSENFELD, SACHIKO ROSENFELD and MOEJOE PROPERTIES LLC'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records Served Upon Custodians Of Records For Old Republic Title Company, Fidelity National Title Company And Pacific Realty Exchange; And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss relevance of documents and financial privacy issues of third parties. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NORTHERN CALIFORNIA COLLECTION SERVICE, INC. VS. WILLIAM FRANCIS ALBER AS AN INDIVIDUAL AND DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renewed Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Set Aside Void Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 9, 2013, Line 2: DEFENDANT WILLIAM ALBER'S Motion To Set Aside Void Default And Default Judgment Defendant's motion to set aside void default and default judgment is granted. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant's motion is timely. The Declaration of Mr. Alber sufficiently demonstrates that he was not properly served with the Summons and Complaint by substituted service. (See Declaration of William Alber in Support of Motion, 7-8.) The present motion is not a motion for reconsideration under CCP § 1008 because the Court did not deny Defendant's motion November 7, 2013; the motion was simply taken off calendar as untimely (CCP §§1005(b); 1013(a).) Defendant shall file its proposed answer and proposed cross-complaint within 5 days after notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted no opposition; sanctions of $1560 per CRC 3:1348. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD C. BROYLES et al VS. TOM'S AIRCRAFT ENTERPRISES A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION et al*****TRANSFERRED TO LAKE COUNTY**
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Transfer Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 9, 2013, Line 15: DEFENDANT TOM WASSON'S Motion For Transfer Of Venue Defendants' motion to transfer venue to Lake County is granted, and Plaintiffs' countermotion to retain jurisdiction in San Francisco is denied. This is a personal injury action, and none of the defendants reside or have their principal place of business in San Francisco. (CCP § 395(a).) The moving defendants reside and have their principal place of business in Lake County, and the other defendants are out-of-state corporations. Venue is proper in Lake County, and the motion to transfer venue was timely made. (CCP §396b.) Defendants filed their motion to transfer at the time they answered the Complaint. As to Plaintiffs' countermotion, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated that this Court should retain jurisdiction based on the convenience of witnesses. (See Witkin, California Procedure, Action § 946, p. 1168.) In their declarations, Plaintiffs' witnesses Bruce Borden, Arin Kumar and Abdon Llorente do not state the nature of the testimony expected. Further and significantly, they have not shown that the inconvenience of traveling to Lake County warrants retaining venue in San Francisco; each witness intends to bill for his travel time. Further, Plaintiff Ronald Broyle has not sufficiently demonstrated that his health would be endangered by traveling to Lake County. Mr. Broyle has not proffered any current medical reports; the last medical report is dated September 6, 2012. No medical professional has opined that Mr. Broyle is too ill or feeble to travel to Lake County without endangering his health. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: LINDA A HESS VS. EXPERIAN CREDIT REPORTING INFO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of And Petition To Compel Arbitation And To Stay The Proceedings;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 9, 2013, Line 16: DEFENDANT FIRST PREMIER BANK'S Petition To Compel Arbitation And To Stay The Proceedings Defendant's petition to compel arbitration is denied. Defendant has not demonstrated that a valid and enforceable agreement to arbitrate exists between the parties. (See CCP §1281.2.) The declaration of Lauri Jackson states that Plaintiff and defendant First Premier Bank entered into a credit card agreement for a certain account ending in 5154. (Jackson Decl. 3.) Defendant does not meet its burden of establishing that this credit card agreement applies to this case. Further, Defendant has not shown that the document attached as Exhibit B to the Jackson Declaration pertains to Plaintiff or this case. (Id. 5.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
